# Comprehensive project clean script

A shell script that cleans all common build artifact directories across multiple language ecosystems:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# clean.sh — removes generated files, preserves all source code
set -euo pipefail

PROJECT_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"

echo "Cleaning build artifacts in: $PROJECT_ROOT"

# JavaScript / TypeScript
rm -rf node_modules dist build out .next .nuxt .svelte-kit

# Turbo / Vercel
rm -rf .turbo .vercel

# Rust
rm -rf target/debug target/incremental

# Python
find . -type d -name __pycache__ -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
find . -name "*.pyc" -delete 2>/dev/null || true
rm -rf .pytest_cache .mypy_cache .ruff_cache

# Test artifacts
rm -f test_output_*.log junit_*.xml coverage.xml

echo "Done. Run your build command to regenerate."
```

Key safety properties:
1. Uses `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` to anchor paths to repo root
2. Every rm target is a relative path from the repo root
3. No wildcards that could expand to system directories
4. Source files (.ts, .rs, .py, .js) are never referenced
